title: GetHtmlParams
description:
Parameters for `tool.get_html`. Either give a tab and (optionally) a snapshot ref to scope the dump to one subtree, or leave both empty to fall back to the Agent Window's active tab and the document root.

The wire field is `"ref"` (a Rust keyword); the Rust binding lives in [`Self::ref_`] and uses serde rename / alias so JSON stays clean.

max_bytes.description: Truncate the HTML to at most this many bytes. Optional; extension applies a sensible default (`524288` = 512 KiB) when omitted.

ref.description: Optional `@e<N>` ref allocated by the last `tool.snapshot`. Accepts both `"e3"` and `"@e3"`. When given the extension calls `DOM.getOuterHTML({ backendNodeId })` for the matching node; otherwise it fetches the document element.
